title: Biological Interplay Between Helicobacter pylori and Epstein–Barr virus in Gastric Epithelial Cell Models
author: Bui, Ngoc-Niem
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) was performed to investigate transcriptomic alterations in AGS and EBV-infected AGS [AGS EBV(+)] gastric epithelial cells following infection with  Helicobacter pylori . Cells were infected with  H. pylori  at multiplicities of infection (MOIs) of 0, 5, and 20, and harvested at 48 h post-infection. Total RNA was extracted from each experimental group, and RNA integrity was assessed prior to library preparation. Sequencing libraries were generated and subjected to high-throughput sequencing to analyze global gene expression profiles. Differentially expressed genes and enriched signaling pathways were subsequently compared between AGS and AGS EBV(+) cells under different  H. pylori  infection conditions to evaluate the impact of bacterial infection and EBV status on host cellular transcriptional responses.
